anzsic_codes.name: Other Auxiliary Finance and Investment Services

merger_description:
Count Limited (**Count**), through its wholly owned subsidiaries, Count Holdings No. 3 Pty Ltd, Count Holdings No. 1 Pty Ltd and Count Holdings No. 4 Pty Ltd, proposes to acquire 100% of the issued shares in Tamarama Accounting Pty Ltd, Tamarama Advisory Pty Ltd and Tamarama Investment Management Pty Ltd (the **Target Companies**) (the **Acquisition**).

Immediately prior to completion, the assets and businesses operated by Oracle Accounting (Australia) Pty Ltd as trustee for the Oracle Accounting Unit Trust (**Oracle Accounting**), Oracle Advisory Group Pty Ltd as trustee for the Oracle Advisory Group Unit Trust (**Oracle Advisory**) and Oracle Investment Management Pty Ltd as trustee for the Farnam Unit Trust (**Oracle Investment Management**) (together, the **Oracle Group**) will be transferred to the respective Target Company.

Count is an ASX listed company comprising an integrated network of over 120 firms providing accounting and wealth management services throughout the country (except for the Northern Territory). Count’s business is organised into three operating segments:

1. equity partnerships (includes investments into firms that provide accounting, audit and assurance, taxation, financial planning services as well as business and corporate advisory services);
2. wealth (includes financial services and investments products provided by Australian Financial Services Licence holders); and
3. services (includes services that support the activities of accounting and financial planning firms that are both internal and external to Count’s network, including actuarial certificates, consulting, IT services, training and helpdesk and support services).

Oracle Group provides financial advice, accounting and investment management services from a network of 14 offices across New South Wales, Queensland and Victoria. Oracle Group operates through three distinct specialist business.

1. Oracle Accounting provides a full-service accounting offering to individuals and businesses, with services including personal and business taxation, bookkeeping, business services, preparation of business activity statements / instalment activity statements, accounting services for self-managed super funds and audit coordination.
2. Oracle Advisory provides independent and holistic financial planning and wealth accumulation strategies to individuals, families and retail and wholesale businesses, with services including investment advice, retirement planning, superannuation, insurance advice, estate planning and lending advice.
3. Oracle Investment Management provides managed investment solutions including discretionary portfolio management across equities, property and fixed income.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Count Limited (Count) proposes to acquire 100% of the issued
shares in Tamarama Accounting Pty Ltd, Tamarama Advisory Pty Ltd
and Tamarama Investment Management Pty Ltd (the Target
Companies) through its wholly owned subsidiaries (Count Holdings
No. 3 Pty Ltd, Count Holdings No. 1 Pty Ltd and Count Holdings No.
4 Pty Ltd), as described in the transaction documents provided as
part of the application (the Acquisition).
Immediately prior to completion, the assets and businesses
operated by Oracle Accounting (Australia) Pty Ltd as trustee for the
Oracle Accounting Unit Trust (Oracle Accounting), Oracle Advisory
Group Pty Ltd as trustee for the Oracle Advisory Group Unit Trust
(Oracle Advisory) and Oracle Investment Management Pty Ltd as
trustee for the Farnam Unit Trust (Oracle Investment Management)
(together, the Oracle Group) will be transferred to the respective
Target Company.
Count Holdings No. 3 Pty Ltd, Count Holdings No. 1 Pty Ltd and
Count Holdings No. 4 Pty Ltd applied for a notification waiver in
respect of the Acquisition.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, Count, is an ASX listed company, comprising an
integrated network of over 120 firms providing accounting and
wealth management services throughout the country (except for the
Northern Territory). Count’s business is organised into three
operating segments:
1. equity partnerships (includes investments into firms that
provide accounting, audit and assurance, taxation, financial
planning services as well as business and corporate
advisory services);
2. wealth (includes financial services and investments
products provided by Australian Financial Services Licence
holders); and
3. services (includes services that support the activities of
accounting and financial planning firms that are both
internal and external to Count’s network, including actuarial
certificates, consulting, IT services, training and helpdesk
and support services).
The target, Oracle Group, provides financial advice, accounting and
investment management services from a network of 14 offices
across New South Wales, Queensland and Victoria. Oracle Group
operates through three distinct specialist business:
1. Oracle Accounting provides a full-service accounting
offering to individuals and businesses, with services
including personal and business taxation, bookkeeping,
business services, preparation of business activity
statements / instalment activity statements, accounting
services for self-managed super funds and audit
coordination;
2. Oracle Advisory provides independent and holistic financial
planning and wealth accumulation strategies to individuals,
families and retail and wholesale businesses, with services
including investment advice, retirement planning,
superannuation, insurance advice, estate planning and
lending advice; and
3. Oracle Investment Management provides managed
investment solutions including discretionary portfolio
management across equities, property and fixed income
Both Count and the Oracle Group supply financial advisory services,
financial investment management services and accounting services.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. the parties’ combined share in the supply of financial
advisory services, financial investment management
services and accounting services in NSW, Victoria,
Queensland and nationally is low and the increase in share
of supply arising from the Acquisition would be small
b. there are alternative suppliers of financial advisory services,
financial investment management services and accounting
services in NSW, Victoria, Queensland (including in the areas
surrounding Oracle Group’s offices) and nationally.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
